---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Sudeep Santhosh"
date: "2026-03-04"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(haven)
library(labelled)
library(sjlabelled)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r}
unzip("data/CRONOS3 Theme - Make it Healthy.zip", list = TRUE)
```

```{r}
healthy=haven::read_sav(here("data","CRONOS3 Theme - Make it Healthy.sav"))
dictionary=labelled::generate_dictionary(healthy)
```

```{r}
view(healthy)
```

check out the data availability for w1hq3(Needed to see medical specialists (excluding dentists and mental health services) last 12 months)
```{r}
ggplot(healthy, aes(x = as_factor(w1hq3))) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Needed to See Medical Specialist (Last 12 Months)",
    x = "Response",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
sum(healthy$w1hq3 == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
```
